analysis.summary: The technician attempted to stop the Apache2 web server, access Nextcloud configuration files, stop a Docker container, and navigate to the Nextcloud directory on a Linux machine. All attempts resulted in errors, indicating that services were not running, directories did not exist, or permission was denied. No clear progress was made in resolving any issues.
actions_taken: Attempted to stop Apache2 web server service (`sudo systemctl stop apache2`)
Attempted to edit Nextcloud configuration file (`sudo nano /var/www/nextcloud/config/config.php`)
Attempted to stop a Docker container named 'nextcloud' (`docker stop nextcloud`)
Performed iSCSI discovery and login attempts (`sudo iscsiadm --mode discovery`, `sudo iscsiadm --mode node --targetname ... --login`)
issues_observed: iSCSI login attempts resulted in 'iscsiadm: No records found'
Apache2 service failed to stop because 'Unit apache2.service not loaded.' (repeated twice)
Nextcloud configuration directory `/var/www/nextcloud/config` did not exist
Stopping Docker container failed with 'permission denied while trying to connect to the docker API at unix:///var/run/docker.sock'
Nextcloud directory `/var/www/nextcloud` did not exist when attempting to `cd` into it
notable_events: Repeated execution of a command (`sudo systemctl stop apache2`) that had previously failed with the same error.
Multiple attempts to interact with services/directories that were not present or accessible.
concerns: The technician made multiple attempts to perform actions without first verifying the existence of services or directories, indicating a lack of systematic troubleshooting.

stuck_details: The technician tried to stop `apache2` twice (at 00:05 and 00:25) despite the initial attempt failing with 'Unit apache2.service not loaded.' This indicates a lack of checking service status before retrying the same command. Each attempt lasted about 2-3 seconds, with roughly 20 seconds between the attempts.

The technician attempted to edit a Nextcloud configuration file and then change into the Nextcloud directory (at 00:12 and 00:17) without verifying their existence. Both actions failed immediately because the directories were not found, suggesting a trial-and-error approach rather than a methodical one.
tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician did not work methodically, repeatedly attempting actions that failed due to fundamental issues (service not loaded, directory not found, permission denied) without prior verification. This led to no observable progress within the session.
coaching_flags: Prioritize checking service status (`systemctl status <service>`) before attempting to stop or restart services.
Verify the existence of directories (`ls -l <path>`) before attempting to navigate into them or edit files within them.
Develop a systematic diagnostic approach when multiple issues or failures are encountered, rather than a sequence of trial-and-error commands.
